title: fix(kpi): fail closed on missing dashboard password
author: fisventurous
pr_body_excerpt:
makes kpi dashboard authentication fail closed if dashboard password is not configured

closes the fail-open posture gap where missing secrets left Stripe revenue and telemetry public, though we can revert if local backward compatibility is preferred

change
\- return a 500 response when `DASHBOARD_PASSWORD` is empty or undefined

gist.headline: Locking the dashboard doors
gist.blurb: The internal KPI dashboard now requires a password to be explicitly set, keeping our telemetry and metrics safely tucked away.
gist.summary: Updated the internal KPI dashboard authentication to fail closed and return a 500 error if the DASHBOARD_PASSWORD environment variable is missing.
gist.impact: Prevents accidental exposure of internal metrics by ensuring the dashboard cannot be accessed without proper configuration.
gist.visual_concept: The pollinations bee putting a sturdy padlock on a glowing retro server cabinet or a dashboard monitor, representing secure-by-default authentication.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot placing a chunky pixel padlock on a glowing retro server cabinet. A cute robot with a CRT monitor head stands nearby holding a golden key. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
